title: How well did Ritsuko know her father
display_title: How well did Ritsuko know her father
wikitext:
[]: How well did Ritsuko know her father?             Quote Post by V » Wed Oct 01, 2008 2:03 am How well did Ritsuko know her father? (if at all?)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 01, 2008 2:16 am Hmm... interesting question. I haven't the slightest idea. Hell, did Naoko even know who Ritsuko's father was? She struck me as being very available in her youth.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Oct 01, 2008 3:00 am The reason I ask is because we only get one brief clue about it, even though its a central part of Ritsuko's character: Naoko says off-hand to Ritsuko in her letter, that Ritsuko did not grow up with the influence of a man. Translations vary. But the way she says it is kind of vague enough to be taken multiple ways: did Naoko leave Ritsuko's father, when Ritsuko was still a baby? (thus getting full custody)? Did her father die? I think it was something "voluntary" on Naoko's part, though, as she says that the lack of male presence was her own fault, as if she concievably had some control over that. Anyway, it seems odd upon rewatching say episode 9 that Ritsuko is giving Misato advice on Kaji, when in fact, Ritsuko has never had a male influence like a father-figure in her life, and has never had a relationship with a man even through college....except of course for shacking up with Gendo after her mother died, but that's a whole other can of worms........ Still, this does seem to tie into the defining aspect of Ritsuko: the brilliant professional who is just living in her mother's shadow. And she's more cold and calculating but really that's just an exterior and she doesn't have a firm control on things either. I know people like Ritsuko. So I don't know how the lack of a father (to whatever degree) affected her: Misato's parents divorced when she was a child, so she at least remembers them being married and it deeply affected her. But Ritsuko does not even remember her father. Does this affect her relationships with men, and other people in general? if so, how? at the least I think it makes "living in her mother's shadow" all the more important, if her only parent was her mother.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 01, 2008 3:59 am V wrote: I think it was something "voluntary" on Naoko's part, though, as she says that the lack of male presence was her own fault, as if she concievably had some control over that.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Oct 01, 2008 7:11 am NemZ wrote: except of course for shacking up with Gendo after her mother died, but that's a whole other can of worms...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 01, 2008 10:41 am Oh no... I'm suggesting he was with Naoko first and he started up with Ritsuko afterwards, with Rits fully aware he was already taken by her mom. Gendo the silver tongued devil sweet talked her into it, but due to her already having a bit of a crush and typical teenage rebellion it didn't take much work. Thus two-timing is actually going the other way, and Naoko was never aware (puts a different spin on the 'old hag' moment as well, ne?). ...yeah, I admit that's total fanwank. So what?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Oct 01, 2008 11:23 am Ritsuko is a test tube baby.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Oct 01, 2008 11:31 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Ritsuko is a test tube baby.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Oct 01, 2008 11:38 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Ritsuko is a test tube baby.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 01, 2008 12:25 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Ritsuko is a test tube baby.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Wed Oct 01, 2008 12:55 pm Gendo's last line to Ritsuko: "I am your father."

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Oct 01, 2008 1:24 pm ^ Noooooo.....that's impossible!

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Oct 01, 2008 1:50 pm I wouldn't doubt it. The sperm doner was then part of the K-Expedition and was killed in the holocaust that happened at the South Pole.(more fanwanking)

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Oct 01, 2008 4:32 pm ^ I didn't really mean it to be the good Dr., but that sure is a great theory as well!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 01, 2008 4:46 pm Action_Bastard wrote: that sure is a great theory as well!

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Oct 01, 2008 4:51 pm I had a feeling you would be disgusted by it, Reichu.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Oct 01, 2008 4:54 pm Maybe in the manga continuity, Gendou is Asuka's real father. Didn't she say he was a "brilliant scientist?" Okay, probably not, but that would be really amusing.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 01, 2008 5:05 pm Lucretius wrote: Maybe in the manga continuity, Gendou is Asuka's real father.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 01, 2008 5:22 pm Oh dear god. It's bad enough with Shinji/Rei (who would be cute together as I admitted before) not being together because of the Yui factor but not Asuka too! @_@ Anyway, from what I understood of the manga, the sperm donor for Kyoko was still named Langley? About Ritsuko: I thought in the manga at least she WAS a test tube baby? Or am I just thinking of Manga-Asuka?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 01, 2008 5:30 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Anyway, from what I understood of the manga, the sperm donor for Kyoko was still named Langley?
